message:
🌱 Add flowise: In Memory Vector Store not working [BUG] mission (#983)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "flowise-2501-in-memory-vector-store-not-working-bug",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "flowise: In Memory Vector Store not working [BUG]",
+    "description": "In Memory Vector Store not working [BUG]. This issue affects 5+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify the issue",
+        "description": "Check your Flowise installation for In-Memory Vector Store errors. The issue manifests as:\n```\nError: Received tool input did not match expected schema\n```\nThis occurs when using the In-Memory Vector Store with certain agent configurations."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review Flowise configuration",
+        "description": "Check your Flowise setup:\n```bash\ndocker logs flowise --tail=50 2>/dev/null | grep -i error\n```\nThe bug is related to how the In-Memory Vector Store component passes schema information to agent tools."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix",
+        "description": "Update Flowise to a version with the fix:\n```bash\n# Via npm\nnpm update flowise -g\n\n# Via Docker\ndocker pull flowiseai/flowise:latest\ndocker restart flowise\n```\nIf you cannot update, try using a different vector store (e.g., Chroma) as a workaround."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the fix",
+        "description": "Test the In-Memory Vector Store by:\n1. Creating a new flow with an In-Memory Vector Store\n2. Upserting a document (e.g., a PDF)\n3. Querying through a Conversational Agent\n\nConfirm the \"tool input did not match expected schema\" error no longer occurs."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The In-Memory Vector Store bug was caused by incorrect schema validation in the DynamicStructuredTool. Updating Flowise resolves the issue.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "flowise",
+      "community",
+      "ai-app",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],

+    },

+  },

+      "npm",
+      "docker"
+    ],

+  },
